pattern: Generating every single-digit-swap of a number's own decimal string (letting a swap collapse into a shorter value through a leading zero) turns 'almost equal' into a plain set-membership check.
hints: A swap happens within ONE number's own digit string, using that number's own natural length - it can never add digits, but it CAN produce a leading zero, which numerically shortens the value (e.g. swapping into '03' gives 3).
For a number x, build the set of every value reachable by swapping any two positions of str(x), plus x itself (zero swaps); do this once per array element.
Two numbers are almost equal iff one appears in the other's reachable set - check both directions, since if the two numbers have different digit lengths only one direction can ever apply.

edge_inputs.description: chain of leading-zero relationships across different lengths
all duplicates, every pair already equal
permutations of the same digits, not all reachable by one swap
extreme length gap: 7-digit vs 1-digit needing a swap across the whole string
mixed lengths where only some pairs are truly reachable
values that share digits but require two swaps, not one

stress.description: random values near max length and max magnitude
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: many small-length duplicate-heavy values to stress tie/duplicate handling
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 99) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
